ddls:
CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region (county or city) */,
  "region_type" TEXT /* Type of region (e.g., county, city) */,
  "parent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent region (for hierarchical relationships) */,
  "region_code" TEXT /* Unique code for each region */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the region */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic indicator */,
  "variable" TEXT /* Short variable name for the indicator */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full descriptive name of the indicator */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the unit of measurement for the indicator */,
  "timeframe_id" INTEGER /* ID of the timeframe for the indicator */,
  "accuracy" TEXT /* Description of the data accuracy and completeness */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the indicator */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source of the indicator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_economic_indicators_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES units ("unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_economic_indicators_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_economic_indicators_timeframe_id FOREIGN KEY ("timeframe_id") REFERENCES timeframes ("timeframe_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the economic indicator being recorded */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region where the data is collected */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the recorded data */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the recorded data */,
  "accuracy_comment" TEXT /* Comments regarding the accuracy and completeness of the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_economic_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_economic_data_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES economic_indicators ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality entry */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the economic data record */,
  "accuracy_comment" TEXT /* Comments regarding the accuracy and completeness of the data */,
  "completeness" TEXT /* Description of data completeness (e.g., complete, missing values) */,
  "validation_status" TEXT /* Validation status of the data (e.g., validated, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES economic_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role */,
  "password" TEXT /* User's password */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_log" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the economic data being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user's device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_log_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES economic_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_log_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "timeframes" (
  "timeframe_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the timeframe */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the timeframe */,
  "start_year" INTEGER /* Starting year of the timeframe */,
  "end_year" INTEGER /* Ending year of the timeframe */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the data collection (e.g., annual, quarterly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("timeframe_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit (e.g., RMB, RMB (million)) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the unit */,
  "symbol" TEXT /* Symbol for the unit (e.g., ¥, ¥M) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., National Statistical Agency) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the economic data that was updated */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the update */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the update was made */,
  "update_comment" TEXT /* Comments regarding the update */,
  "update_type" TEXT /* Type of update (e.g., insert, update, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_updates_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_updates_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES economic_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audits" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed (e.g., login, logout, data access) */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date when the action was performed */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details about the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who provided feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date when the feedback was provided */,
  "message" TEXT /* Feedback message from the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
